reasoning: The baseline rate of a true pandemic declaration is rare (roughly once every 10-15 years). With about 5 months left in 2025, this sets a very low base rate (~3%). There are currently no diseases with sustained, newly spreading human-to-human transmission that would trigger a WHO pandemic declaration. H5N1 human cases remain rare and do not exhibit efficient human-to-human spread as of early August 2025. Mpox is a PHEIC but the WHO has historically avoided calling it a 'pandemic'. Furthermore, while May 2025 WHO IHR amendments created a new formal "pandemic emergency" mechanism, it seems unlikely to be invoked for the first time without a sudden and severe global outbreak on the scale of COVID-19, particularly given political headwinds like the July 2025 US withdrawal from the WHO pandemic response reforms. Given this lack of epidemiological triggers and only ~5 months remaining in the year, the probability is near base rate, although slightly elevated by the general global tension around H5N1. I estimate the probability at 4%.
